title: Chrysanthemum maresii
taxonomy: Chrysanthemum maresii is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Asteraceae. It is a type of chrysanthemum that is native to China and is known for its unique characteristics. The classification of Chrysanthemum maresii is further divided into several subcategories, including the genus Chrysanthemum and the tribe Anthemideae. This plant is closely related to other chrysanthemum species and is often studied alongside them in botanical research. As a member of the Asteraceae family, Chrysanthemum maresii has distinct features that set it apart from other flowering plants.

intro: Chrysanthemum maresii, also known as Mares' treacle or Yellow chrysanthemum, is a plant species in the daisy family that has been traditionally used in herbal medicine. The health benefits of Chrysanthemum maresii include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, such as rutin and quercetin, as well as sesquiterpenes and other phenolic compounds. Chrysanthemum maresii is commonly prepared as a tea, infusions, or tinctures, and is used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat various conditions, including fever, coughs, and bronchitis.
